{"key_event":"Conservative backlash and liberal parliamentary resurgence","reasoning":"The Napoleonic administrative apparatus, police powers, censorship episodes, and 1823 Spanish expedition kept the Bourbon state strong, though the 1827–1828 reversals showed real political limits. Civil society ended somewhat stronger than in 1818 because press networks, elections, petitions, bourgeois notables, and parliamentary opposition deepened despite repression of radicals and clerical-ultra pressure.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":6.2,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":7.5}
